summary: Finished matching Peach's down-special turnip-pluck file ftPe_SpecialLw by replacing the assembly fallback with the C object in the build, marking the source as Matching, deleting asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_SpecialLw.s, and reshaping spawnVeg with small helper functions for turnip-kind selection and item setup.

key_files.role: C source updated to match; spawnVeg was refactored and helper functions getVeg/setupVeg were added.
Deleted 457-line assembly fallback after the C source matched.
Changed ftPe_SpecialLw.c from NonMatching to Matching.

what_changed: Removed the entire assembly implementation for ftPe_SpecialLw.s, including local helpers pickVeg, spawnVeg, handleAirColl, handleColl, and global Peach down-special entry/anim/phys/coll routines.
Changed obj_files.mk so the build links the C object for ftPe_SpecialLw instead of the asm object.
Added static ItemKind getVeg(HSD_GObj* gobj), which reads Peach dat_attrs x14, defaults to It_Kind_Peach_Turnip, and calls pickVeg(gobj) when HSD_Randi(da_x14) returns 0.

Added static void setupVeg(ItemKind kind, HSD_GObj* gobj, Fighter* fp, Vec3* pos), which calls it_802BD4AC, writes fp->item_gobj and fp->fv.pe.veg_gobj, then on success calls ftCo_80094818, efSync_Spawn(1234, gobj, &fp->cur_pos), and installs ftPe_Init_OnDeath2 into death2_cb and take_dmg_cb.
Simplified spawnVeg into a top-level ftCheckThrowB0(fp) guard followed by lb_8000B1CC position lookup, getVeg(gobj), and setupVeg(kind, gobj, fp, &pos).
spawnVeg gained local Vec3 pos, p1, and p2 declarations; p1 and p2 are not used in the diff, so they appear to be stack-shaping locals rather than gameplay logic.
smart_moves: Factored turnip random-selection logic into getVeg, separating the HSD_Randi/dat_attrs decision from item-spawn side effects.
Factored item creation and callback/effect setup into setupVeg, reducing nested scopes in spawnVeg while preserving the order of writes to fp->item_gobj and fp->fv.pe.veg_gobj.
Kept the build-system changes in the same PR as the source match: configure.py was flipped to Matching and obj_files.mk was flipped to the C object only after deleting the asm fallback.

Preserved the observed Peach turnip item flow: pick a kind, create it via it_802BD4AC, cache the resulting item gobj in both fighter item fields, spawn effect 1234, and set death/damage callbacks on successful spawn.
decomp_lessons: For fighter action files, finishing a match requires both the C code and build metadata: change configure.py from NonMatching to Matching and switch obj_files.mk from asm .s.o to src .c.o.
Small static helpers can improve matching by changing inlining and stack/register behavior while making source intent clearer; here getVeg and setupVeg replaced a deeply nested spawnVeg block.
Unused local Vec3 declarations may be legitimate matching artifacts when trying to reproduce stack layout; document or keep them only when the diff/build evidence shows they are needed for the match.

When removing an asm fallback, ensure all routines formerly present in the assembly file still exist in C or are already covered: this file included Peach down-special Enter, Air Enter, Anim, Phys, Coll, item cleanup, and local collision helpers.

For item-owning fighter moves, preserve cleanup and callback state carefully: this diff keeps item_gobj/fv.pe.veg_gobj synchronization and assigns ftPe_Init_OnDeath2 to both death2_cb and take_dmg_cb after successful spawn.
naming_conventions: Global Peach fighter functions use the ftPe_SpecialLw_* and ftPe_SpecialAirLw_* prefixes, e.g. ftPe_SpecialLw_8011CFA0, ftPe_SpecialLw_Enter, ftPe_SpecialAirLw_Anim.
Local helper functions in this file use lowerCamelCase names such as pickVeg, spawnVeg, getVeg, setupVeg, handleAirColl, and handleColl.
Peach-specific fighter variables are accessed through fp->fv.pe, as shown by fp->fv.pe.veg_gobj.
Item kinds use the ItemKind type and It_Kind_* enum-style constants, specifically It_Kind_Peach_Turnip in this diff.
Existing unknown data fields remain offset-style or placeholder-style where not yet fully named, e.g. ftPe_DatAttrs x14 and fp->ft_data->x8->unk10.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the 457-line asm fallback only after the C source was good enough to be marked Matching.
Moved the file from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py and from asm object to C object in obj_files.mk, which is the standard finalization step for a matched translation unit.
Refactored nested logic in spawnVeg into static helpers, likely to influence compiler output and register/stack behavior while preserving side-effect order.
Added unused Vec3 p1 and Vec3 p2 locals alongside Vec3 pos in spawnVeg; based only on the diff, these appear to be stack-layout padding or matching scaffolding.

Kept calls and ordering visible from the assembly equivalent: lb_8000B1CC for joint position, HSD_Randi/pickVeg for item kind, it_802BD4AC for item creation, ftCo_80094818 and efSync_Spawn after non-null item creation.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that use unused Vec3 locals or padding locals to match fighter stack layout.
Search for Peach turnip/veg cleanup patterns involving fp->item_gobj and fp->fv.pe.veg_gobj.
Compare other fighter special move PRs that split nested spawn logic into static helper functions before marking a file Matching.
Look for later naming improvements to ftPe_DatAttrs.x14 or fp->ft_data->x8->unk10 related to Peach turnip odds.
